C000.summary: P001 is solved. The new test covers the full Runtime preparation path and proves a following `system` message does not suppress current-round display image injection.

Failure mode: `system` follows display tool and pending image is never injected; Runtime full-path test fails.

C003.summary: The root problem is solved. The fix does not rely on the previous "old log" explanation alone: it adds executable guardrails at all three boundaries where the issue could recur.

P000.description:
The current production investigation showed an old LLM request where a successful `display` tool result appeared in the Factory request body as `{"_mcp_content":[{"type":"text","text":"OK"}]}` and no provider-native image message was sent. Even if the currently deployed Cortex endpoint can now format that same step as image content, the system still lacks a hard end-to-end guard that prevents current-round `display` output from silently degrading into `OK` or text-only content.

Fix the runtime/Cortex/Factory boundary so current-round `display` image output either becomes provider-native visual input in the LLM request or fails loudly with diagnosable evidence. The fix must also prove that a following `system` message does not suppress image injection.
success_criteria: Current-round `display` step content containing image `_mcp_content` is converted into a provider-native image message before Factory transport.
A following `system` message after the display tool result does not prevent image injection.
`display_perception` projection must not silently return text-only `OK` for display steps that have media payloads.
Factory log/request snapshot should make image delivery inspectable without dumping raw base64 into the UI.
Tests cover the full Runtime preparation path, not only isolated helper functions.
Old or misleading fallback behavior is removed or guarded where it could hide this bug.
P000.title: Display image must reach LLM as visual input

P001.description: The Runtime helper tests prove isolated `process_multimodal_messages` behavior, but the bug class occurs across the full LLM preparation path: step-ref expansion, sanitization, multimodal conversion, and message ordering. Add a full-path guard so current-round `display` images become provider-native image messages even when a `system` message follows.
success_criteria: A test starts from messages containing a display `step_ref` and fake Cortex bridge output with image `_mcp_content`.
The final messages contain `assistant`, text-only `tool`, inserted `user(image_url)`, then following `system`.
The text-only tool receipt has a placeholder and no raw image base64.
P001.title: Runtime must inject display images through full LLM preparation

P002.description: Factory request logs currently store whatever request body is sent. If provider-native image data is present, logs/UI can become huge and misleading, while if image data is missing the UI does not make that absence explicit enough. Add log-only redaction so image delivery remains inspectable without dumping raw base64.
success_criteria: Factory log snapshot keeps provider transport untouched.
Logged messages replace `image_url.url` data URLs with metadata placeholders.
Logged messages replace Anthropic-style image source data with metadata placeholders.
Tests prove raw base64 is absent from the logged request body while image markers remain visible.
P002.title: Factory logs must show image delivery without raw base64

P003.description: Cortex `display_perception` projection currently uses a generic fallback that can produce `{"type":"text","text":"OK"}` when no text or display media is found. For display perception this is too ambiguous: it can hide a broken media projection and make Factory logs look successful while no image was delivered.
success_criteria: `display_perception` projection for empty parsed content returns a diagnostic text marker instead of plain `OK`.
Normal history/current tool projections can keep their existing text behavior if needed.
Tests prove empty display projection is distinguishable from a successful image projection.
P003.title: Cortex display projection must not hide missing media as OK

done: Verified the tool receipt is text-only with an image placeholder and no raw base64.
R000.summary: Added a Runtime full-path regression test that starts from a display `step_ref`, expands it through a fake Cortex bridge, runs `prepare_llm_call`, and proves current-round display image data becomes a provider-native `user(image_url)` message even when a `system` message follows the tool result.

Added structured redaction for Anthropic `image.source.data` base64.
Preserved image metadata such as MIME type, base64 length, and non-secret image fields.
Added tests for both provider shapes.
R001.summary: Implemented Factory log-only media redaction in `build_chat_log_snapshot` so provider-native image payloads remain inspectable without storing raw base64 in request logs.

done: `format_for_display_perception_llm` now uses `[Display produced no text or media for LLM perception]` when no text/media is available.
Added a unit test for empty display projection.
R002.summary: Changed Cortex display perception formatting so an empty display projection emits a diagnostic text marker instead of ambiguous `OK`, while preserving image projection behavior.
verification: Initial run without dependency PYTHONPATH failed on missing `logicalfs`/`sandbox_sdk`; reran with required local package paths.

done: Runtime now has a full `prepare_llm_call` regression ensuring `display` step-ref image output becomes provider-native `user(image_url)` even before a following `system` message.
Factory log snapshots now redact OpenAI and Anthropic provider-native image base64 while preserving visible image metadata.
Cortex display perception empty fallback now emits a diagnostic marker instead of ambiguous `OK`.
known_gaps: Final cross-repo focused test rerun and diff review still need to be performed before root success check.
R003.summary: Completed the split ticket by closing all three child problems: Runtime full-path image injection, Factory log image redaction, and Cortex display projection diagnostics.
verification: P003 / R002: `PYTHONPATH=.:../novaic-logicalfs:../novaic-common:../novaic-sandbox-sdk pytest -q tests/test_step_result_projection.py tests/test_tool_output_projection.py` -> `13 passed`.

acceptance_criteria: Runtime `prepare_llm_call` converts a current-round `display` step-ref image into a `user` message with `image_url`.
Runtime preserves the image injection when a `system` message follows the display tool result.
Factory request logging redacts image data into metadata rather than storing raw base64.
Cortex display projection no longer hides empty display projection as a plain `OK` without diagnostics.
Focused tests pass in the touched repos.
assumptions: `display` is the only direct tool that should produce current-turn visual input.
Shell remains terminal-text only and should not directly inject images.
It is acceptable for Factory logs to show placeholders for image data instead of raw base64.
T000.classification_reason: Touches Runtime preparation, Cortex projection, and Factory logging; each has a separate failure mode and verification path.
T000.problem_definition: Current-round `display` tool output can be visually valid in Cortex but still fail to reach the model as provider-native image input if any boundary formats, sanitizes, or logs it incorrectly. The observed failure mode was a `display` tool result becoming text-only `OK` in the Factory request body, with no extra `user(image_url)` message.
T000.proposed_solution:
Add an explicit end-to-end contract across the Runtime preparation path:

- Keep the Cortex projection contract strict for `display_perception`: image payloads must remain image blocks, while missing image data should be visible as a diagnostic text receipt instead of an ambiguous `OK`.
- Add Runtime tests that start from LLM input messages with a display `step_ref`, run `prepare_llm_call`, and assert the final messages include a provider-native image message even when a `system` message follows the tool result.
- Add Factory log snapshot redaction so raw base64 is not dumped into logs/UI, while preserving a clear inspectable marker that an image was sent.
- Run focused Runtime/Cortex/Factory tests and inspect diffs for legacy or misleading fallbacks.
risks: Provider request format must still receive real image data; redaction must apply only to logs, not transport.
Over-tightening the display projection fallback could break text-only `display` outputs, so the test should distinguish image-capable display from normal text.
T000.title: Harden display image LLM injection
T000.verification_plan:
- Add/adjust unit tests for Runtime full preparation path.
- Add/adjust unit tests for Factory log snapshot redaction.
- Add/adjust Cortex projection tests for display projection fallback clarity.
- Run the focused test files.
- Run `git diff --stat` and review the touched diffs.

acceptance_criteria: The test proves raw image base64 is removed from the tool text receipt.
The test proves the inserted user image message is present even with a following system message.
assumptions: A fake bridge is sufficient to represent Cortex `read_step_formatted`.
T001.classification_reason: Focused regression test only, small and directly verifiable.
T001.problem_definition: Runtime has helper-level image conversion tests, but not a full `prepare_llm_call` test covering Cortex step-ref expansion plus `system` ordering.
T001.proposed_solution: Add a focused unit test that monkeypatches the Cortex bridge to return image `_mcp_content`, calls `prepare_llm_call` with a current-round display tool step, and asserts the provider-ready messages include an inserted `user(image_url)` before the following `system` message.
risks: Minimal production risk because this is a regression test first.
T001.title: Add full Runtime display image preparation regression
T001.verification_plan:
- Run the touched Runtime test file.
- Run the broader Runtime image/step-ref test set.

acceptance_criteria: OpenAI `image_url` data URLs are redacted in logs.
Anthropic `image.source.data` base64 is redacted in logs.
Redaction does not alter the provider transport request, only the logged snapshot.
Tests prove raw payload strings are absent and image metadata/markers remain.
assumptions: Factory request logging is built exclusively through `build_chat_log_snapshot`.
T002.classification_reason: Small log-only contract change with direct unit tests.
T002.problem_definition: Factory stores request bodies for debugging. Once Runtime correctly injects provider-native image messages, those logs can contain raw base64 image data, making the UI noisy and hiding whether image delivery actually occurred.
T002.proposed_solution: Add a log-only redaction step inside `build_chat_log_snapshot` that preserves message structure and image metadata while replacing base64 payloads with explicit placeholders.
risks: Redaction should not remove ordinary text or tool schema data.
T002.title: Redact provider-native image data in Factory log snapshots

acceptance_criteria: Empty display perception output is not `OK`.
Existing image display projection remains unchanged.
History/current generic projections are not unnecessarily changed.
assumptions: `OK` can remain for non-display empty projections.
T003.classification_reason: Small projection fallback change with direct unit tests.
T003.problem_definition: `format_for_display_perception_llm` uses a generic empty fallback that emits `OK`. For a display projection, this can hide that no media was actually available for injection.
T003.proposed_solution: Parameterize the MCP content formatter's empty fallback and make `format_for_display_perception_llm` emit a diagnostic display-specific text marker when parsed content has no text and no display media.
risks: Diagnostic text appears to the model when display had no readable content, but that is preferable to silent success.
T003.title: Make empty display perception diagnostic
